question: Implement a C++ function `is_palindrome` that takes a `std::string` as input and returns `true` if the string is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backward, case-sensitive), and `false` otherwise. Test it with several example strings.

test_cases.input: Able was I ere I saw Elba

errors: The function creates a reversed copy of the input string, which incurs O(N) additional space complexity and O(N) time for copying.
A more efficient approach would be to use two pointers (one from each end) to compare characters in-place, achieving O(1) space complexity.
